div.h2: -ERESTARTSYS Signal Flow: Blocked read() → Userspace errno

p.em: How Ctrl+C reaches userspace — and what goes wrong when drivers get it wrong

div.p: Signal Flow Legend → Animated red: SIGINT delivery path → Animated yellow: -ERESTARTSYS propagation → Green: SA_RESTART transparent restart → Orange: SA_RESTART absent → EINTR to userspace ╌ Blue dashed: spurious wakeup loop-back ✕ Red dashed: BUG paths

g.text: Process P: blocked in read(fd, buf, N)

text.tspan: ① SIGINT queued for process P

div.p: Effect: read() returns 0 → userspace thinks file ended (EOF). Signal handler still runs. Process confused about read state. cat exits silently printing nothing. Data lost.
Effect: Process enters D state (uninterruptible sleep). SIGKILL is ignored. Process cannot be killed. Machine may require reboot if many such processes accumulate. Visible in ps aux as state D.
Effect: Programs using SA_RESTART expect transparent restart. Returning -EINTR bypasses the restart mechanism entirely. Every signal causes a visible EINTR even with SA_RESTART set.
